part_1_executive_summary.tagline: Enterprise model registry and governance platform for data science teams
part_1_executive_summary.overview: ModelVault is a cloud-based repository for managing, versioning, and deploying ML models in enterprise environments. Targets mid-market companies (100-1000 employees) with 5+ data scientists struggling with model governance, lineage tracking, and reproducibility.
part_1_executive_summary.problem: Enterprise data science teams lack centralized management for ML models. Model drift, version control chaos, and compliance violations are common. Current solutions require $50K+ annual licenses and custom deployment.
part_1_executive_summary.solution: SaaS platform providing model registry with automatic versioning, lineage tracking, governance controls, and integration with popular ML frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow, JAX).
part_1_executive_summary.market_opportunity: TAM: $3.2B (enterprise ML ops market). SAM: $320M (mid-market segment). SOM: $5.2M (0.3% market share in year 3)
part_1_executive_summary.fit_rationale: Strong technical background in ML/infrastructure. Deep understanding of pain points from FAANG experience. Can build MVP independently. Primary concern: zero GTM experience.

target_customer.persona: ML Platform Lead at mid-market tech company
pain_points: Model version chaos (which model version is in production?)
Reproducibility issues (can we rebuild this model?)
Governance gaps (who deployed what model and why?)
Compliance risk (audit trails, data lineage for regulated industries)
target_customer.decision_timeline: 2-4 weeks (technical lead proposes, VP Engineering approves)

direct_competitors: Weights & Biases ($50K+/year, experiment tracking + model registry)
competitive_landscape.market_gap: SaaS model registry between free open-source and $100K+ enterprise suites. Target companies outgrowing MLflow but not ready for full-stack platforms.

market_trends: 2024-2025: Rapid adoption of ML in mid-market (AI-powered features)
Growing compliance burden (EU AI Act, SOX for finance)
Shift from 'let data scientists do whatever' to 'governed ML ops'
Strong VC funding in ML infrastructure ($2B+ invested in 2024-2025)
part_2_market_analysis.arbitration_rationale: Clear market signal, underserved middle market, founder expertise aligns well. Risk: lengthy enterprise sales cycles (8-12 weeks). Mitigation: focus on product-led growth initially.

conservative_annual_projection.month_13_24: 10 customers @ avg $1500/mo = $18K MRR by end of year 1
conservative_annual_projection.month_25_36: 40 customers @ avg $2200/mo = $88K MRR by end of year 2
conservative_annual_projection.month_37_48: 80 customers @ avg $2800/mo = $224K MRR by end of year 3

part_3_financial_model.financial_rationale: Startup costs well within available capital. Gross margins 92%. Can reach break-even with just 1 customer. Path to profitability realistic within 12 months.

tactics: Write 12-15 blog posts on ML model governance, versioning best practices
Publish on Medium, dev.to, MLOps forums (Reddit r/MachineLearning, etc.)
Speak at 2-3 local meetups (Austin ML community, PyTorch meetups)
Build small GitHub presence (open-source MLflow extensions, comparison tools)
Launch MVP with 14-day free trial (no credit card required)
Offer free tier for open-source projects (builds goodwill, network effects)
Run webinars for target customers (MLOps best practices)
Collect 50 free trial signups by end of month 6
Outbound to 20 high-fit companies (> 50 employees, visible ML team)
Target decision-makers (ML Platform Leads, VP Engineering)
Aim for 5-10 initial customer conversations by month 12

part_4_go_to_market.gtm_rationale: Strong product-led growth potential. Founder has zero GTM experience—critical risk. Mitigation: cofound with sales/product person by month 6, or hire fractional VP Sales.

deliverables: Interview 20 ML engineers about model governance pain points
Define MVP scope: model upload + versioning + lineage view only
phase_0_validation.success_criteria: 15+ signups for beta, 80%+ of interviewees report 'urgent' problem

deliverables: Core model registry (upload, version, tag models)
Basic lineage tracking (which datasets → which models)
Governance controls (RBAC, approval workflows, audit log)
Hiring first full-time employee (VP Sales / customer success)
Enterprise features (SAML, compliance certifications, on-prem option)
phase_3_scaling.funding_decision: Series A conversation begins (aim for $2-3M to hire team)

top_3_risks.failure_scenario: Founder unable to acquire initial customers; stuck in 'build vs. sell' paradox
top_3_risks.impact: No revenue by month 12; runway exhausted; project abandoned
top_3_risks.founder_vulnerability: Strong at building, zero sales experience. Typical engineer problem: 'if we build it, they will come.'
early_warning_signs: Month 6: Still zero paying customers despite 100+ free trial users
Month 9: Inbound interest stalled; no sales conversations
Month 10: Using all time on 'one more feature' instead of customer outreach

mitigation.immediate: Co-found or hire fractional VP Sales by month 4. Commit 50% of time to customer conversations (not coding) by month 6.
mitigation.low_cost: Use founder network aggressively. Ask previous managers for intros to ML Platform Leads at their new companies. Target 5 outbound meetings/week by month 5.
mitigation.success_metric: 5+ customer conversations by month 8; 1 paying customer pilot by month 10

top_3_risks.failure_scenario: Technical debt accumulates; MVP bloats; shipping slows to crawl
top_3_risks.impact: Feature-complete competitors gain traction while ModelVault is still in beta
top_3_risks.founder_vulnerability: Perfectionist engineer tendency. Easy to over-engineer model registry (could spent months on 'perfect' architecture).
early_warning_signs: Month 4: MVP still not launched; still architecting
Month 8: Feature list keeps growing; launch keeps slipping

mitigation.immediate: Ruthlessly scope MVP to ONLY: upload model + version + basic lineage view. That's it.
mitigation.weekly: Time-box features to 1-week sprints. If feature takes >5 days, defer to Phase 2.
mitigation.contractor_help: Hire frontend contractor for UI (month 3). Founder focuses only on backend model versioning logic.
mitigation.success_metric: MVP launched by month 10; public beta with 50 signups by month 11

top_3_risks.failure_scenario: H1B visa sponsorship issues; cannot legally work on startup
top_3_risks.impact: Entire venture blocked; no way to proceed
top_3_risks.founder_vulnerability: Currently on H1B but startup is 'side project.' Needs to clarify legal status with immigration attorney.
early_warning_signs: H1B employer becomes aware of startup work; threatens to revoke sponsorship
Immigration attorney advises cannot work on startup while on H1B
Visa application renewal denied; startup mentioned in background check

mitigation.CRITICAL (month 1): Consult immigration attorney immediately. Cost: ~$1500. Options: (a) work on startup part-time and disclose to employer, (b) delay launch until green card approved, (c) switch to E-2 investor visa.
mitigation.legal_structure: If proceeding: structure as LLC with you as employee + small salary ($500/mo) to establish legitimacy.
mitigation.decision_point: If attorney says 'risky,' pause project until visa situation resolved. Not worth jeopardizing immigration status.

survival_readiness.founder_commitment_concern: Will founder actually dedicate enough time while on H1B? Previous pattern suggests part-time projects don't scale.
survival_readiness.recommendation: Founder should plan 3-month sabbatical (month 4-6 of project) or negotiate part-time arrangement with current employer to accelerate customer traction.

part_6_risk_mitigation.approval_rationale: Strong market opportunity, founder expertise aligns well, financial model works. Primary risks are: (1) founder needs to get serious about GTM by month 4, (2) visa situation must be clarified immediately, (3) must resist feature-creep and ship MVP by month 10.
next_steps: Week 1: Consult immigration attorney about H1B + startup legality
Week 2: Validate 20 customer conversations (record pain points)
Week 3: Decide on co-founder/fractional sales hire timeline
Week 4: Set up legal entity (LLC), register domain, deploy landing page
